title: Research monoliths vs microservices
description: Write a comprehensive background and context document covering the key architectural tradeoffs between monolithic and microservice architectures. Include: scalability, deployment complexity, code organization, team structure, operational overhead, fault isolation, technology coupling, and typical use cases for each approach.

title: Analyze and rank tradeoffs
description: Critically evaluate the research findings. Rank the key tradeoffs by impact and relevance. Assess which factors favor monoliths vs microservices, under what conditions. Identify nuances and dependencies between tradeoffs. Prepare a structured evaluation that synthesis can use to craft a tight brief.

title: Write the architectural brief
description: Produce a tight, polished brief (800–1200 words) summarizing the major tradeoffs between monoliths and microservices. Distill the analysis into clear, actionable insights. Structure it for decision-makers: trade-off landscape, when each approach wins, practical guidance. Cite the research and analysis behind every claim.
